name: Autonomous urban passenger-miles share
whyItMatters: Direct read-through on whether thesis timing for city-first deployment is accurate.
statusPlaceholder: Placeholder: tracking operator disclosures and third-party estimates (2026 Q1 baseline).
interpretation: Rising share in target metros supports scale economics for fleet operators; stagnation suggests regulatory or unit-economics bottlenecks.

name: Accident frequency trends (ADS vs. human baseline)
whyItMatters: Validates or challenges the safety narrative that underpins liability and insurance repricing.
statusPlaceholder: Placeholder: awaiting annual NHTSA-aligned cohort update.
interpretation: Improving ADS-relative frequency supports liability shift thesis; deterioration forces reassessment of deployment pace assumptions.

name: Insurer personal-auto loss ratio trajectory
whyItMatters: Links technology outcomes to P&C earnings and reserve behavior.
interpretation: Stable or worsening loss ratios alongside AV miles growth may indicate lagging pricing power or severity surprises.

name: Regulatory milestones (domains, permits, incidents)
whyItMatters: Governance is often the binding constraint on urban scale.
interpretation: Net expansion of operating domains is bullish for deployment; high-profile incidents with restrictive responses are bearish.

name: Used passenger vehicle residual value curves
whyItMatters: Proxy for ownership demand and fleet replacement incentives as utilization models shift.
interpretation: Structural softening in urban segments supports utilization-shift thesis; broad strength suggests ownership preference resilience.

name: Fleet utilization (miles per vehicle per day)
whyItMatters: Unit economics for autonomous ride-hail hinge on utilization, not technology alone.
interpretation: Sustained utilization growth without price destruction supports base case; utilization flat with discounting suggests oversupply.
